tweets.title:
General N. S. Raja Subramani Takes Charge as Chief of Defence Staff

With a career starting in 1985 in the Garhwal Rifles, the new CDS brings extensive experience from operational roles in Jammu & Kashmir and Assam, alongside senior positions as Vice Chief of the Army Staff and Military Adviser to the NSCS. He is highly decorated, having received the PVSM, AVSM, SM, and VSM for his service.

General Subramani’s mandate focuses on critical reforms within the Indian Armed Forces:
- Theaterisation: Implementing integrated military commands.
- Jointness: Enhancing synergies across the Army, Navy, and Air Force.
-Indigenization: Accelerating Atmanirbharta (self-reliance) in defense production.
tweets.content:
General N. S. Raja Subramani Takes Charge as Chief of Defence Staff

With a career starting in 1985 in the Garhwal Rifles, the new CDS brings extensive experience from operational roles in Jammu & Kashmir and Assam, alongside senior positions as Vice Chief of the Army Staff and Military Adviser to the NSCS. He is highly decorated, having received the PVSM, AVSM, SM, and VSM for his service.

General Subramani’s mandate focuses on critical reforms within the Indian Armed Forces:
- Theaterisation: Implementing integrated military commands.
- Jointness: Enhancing synergies across the Army, Navy, and Air Force.
-Indigenization: Accelerating Atmanirbharta (self-reliance) in defense production.

- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● CENTCOM, via the UKMTO/JMIC, issued an urgent maritime advisory north of Oman’s Musandam Peninsula, warning mariners of imminent US naval operations to counter suspected Iranian mine-laying.

● In Washington, President Trump reviewed the draft peace deal with top security advisers in the Situation Room. The session concluded with zero announcements, keeping the region in an uneasy gridlock.

● Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated the U.S. possesses more than sufficient stockpiles to immediately resume aggressive bombardment strikes if Tehran rejects core U.S. parameters, though officials maintain the ceasefire "is not officially over."

● Iranian FM Spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei confirmed text is not finalized. Tehran remains strictly focused on ending hostilities and lifting the U.S. naval blockade, refusing to negotiate the finer details of its nuclear plan at this stage.

● Hezbollah launched a major rocket barrage deep into northern Israel targeting Safed and Kiryat Shmona. In response, the IDF issued immediate evacuation orders for 7 southern Lebanese villages as ground operations intensify.

● The IRGC Navy claimed that 20 commercial vessels successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz over the past 24 hours in direct coordination with Iranian forces.

END OF THE BRIEF

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
29th May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated Imad Hassan Hussein Aslim, the Deputy Commander of the Gaza Brigade and the Commander of the Zeitoun Battalion in Hamas' Military Wing.

● Speaker of the Iranian Parliament Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated that Iran will not take any action on current issues until the US makes the first move.

● Washington explicitly rejected a proposal from Muscat that would allow a joint Iran-Oman authority to levy commercial shipping fees in the Strait of Hormuz.

● Declassified satellite imagery confirmed 4 structural impacts from yesterday’s Iranian missile strike on Ali Al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait. CENTCOM stated the base remains fully operational.

● The bulk carrier MDL KAMRAN successfully crossed the Strait of Hormuz en route to Imam Khomeini port after returning from Brazil.

● As of May 29, CENTCOM has redirected 115 commercial vessels to ensure no commerce enters or leaves Iranian ports.

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: The current market demand is not for “Made in India” but for “Made in Pakistan.”: Bilawal Bhutto

A country that struggles to produce even an indigenous 100cc motorcycle engine is talking about building fighter jets and defence products. Simply putting a “Made in Pakistan” label(even the label printed in China) does not make a defence product truly indigenous.

A few days back, Pakistan’s Defence Minister Khawaja Asif also claimed that selling fighter jets would clear all loans and turn the country’s economy into a top-tier global economy.

How long will they keep selling such churan to their awam ?

Video credit: ARY news

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: सप्त शक्ति कमान के सेना कमांडर लेफ्टिनेंट जनरल मंजिंदर सिंह ने की DOT डिवीजन की युद्धक तैयारियों की समीक्षा, स्वदेशी सैन्य नवाचारों का किया अवलोकन, अत्याधुनिक काउंटर-ड्रोन ट्रेनिंग नोड का उद्घाटन कर भविष्य की चुनौतियों के लिए सेना को किया और मजबूत

In a significant boost to the Indian Army's modernization and operational preparedness efforts, Army Commander of Sapta Shakti Command, Lt Gen Manjinder Singh, conducted a comprehensive review of the operational readiness of DOT Division. The visit highlighted the Army's commitment to enhancing combat effectiveness through innovation, indigenous technology, and advanced military training.

During the interaction, troops showcased a range of indigenous innovations developed to improve battlefield performance, force protection, surveillance capabilities, and mission effectiveness. These soldier-driven technological solutions reflect the Indian Army's growing focus on self-reliance and innovation under the vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat.

A major highlight of the visit was the inauguration of a state-of-the-art Counter Unmanned Aerial Systems (C-UAS) Training Node. The facility will play a crucial role in preparing soldiers to detect, track, and neutralize emerging drone threats, a capability that has become increasingly important in modern warfare and border security operations.

Lt Gen Manjinder Singh commended the professionalism, dedication, and innovative spirit displayed by all ranks. Recognizing exceptional contributions, he also presented awards to deserving personnel for their outstanding performance and commitment to operational excellence.

The visit underscores the Indian Army's continued transformation into a future-ready force capable of addressing evolving security challenges through technology, innovation, and superior combat preparedness. As drone warfare, AI-enabled systems, and next-generation battlefield technologies reshape modern conflicts, initiatives like these ensure that the Indian Army remains prepared, adaptive, and mission-ready.

#IndianArmy #SaptaShaktiCommand #LtGenManjinderSingh #DOTDivision #MilitaryInnovation #AtmanirbharBharat #CounterDrone #CUAS #DefenceNews #MilitaryTechnology #OperationalReadiness #ArmyModernization #IndianDefence #NationalSecurity #FutureReadyForce #DroneWarfare #MilitaryLeadership #CombatReadiness #DefenceJournalism #JaiHind

tweets.title:
OSINT ASSESSMENT:

Operation Sindoor left Pakistan with a serious deep scar. They claimed strikes deep inside India but failed to produce a single credible satellite image to support those claims.

Now comes PRSC-EO3.

Looks like the real mission is not surveillance, but content creation for the next press conference. If Op Sindoor 2.0 ever happens, expect plenty of fake satellite imagery for domestic consumption, regardless of what actually happened on the ground.

Some countries build capabilities for warfare. Others build capabilities to explain away its outcome.

Interestingly, PRSC-EO3 was launched on April 25, 2026, with the help of China's Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center, barely a year after Operation Sindoor exposed the credibility gap in Pakistan's battlefield claims.
tweets.content:

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: An Enduring Legacy

Though India is gradually replacing liquid-fueled Prithvi variants with faster-reacting, solid-fueled systems such as Prahaar and Pralay, the missile remains a landmark achievement. Produced in series by Bharat Dynamics Limited and deployed across multiple regiments, Prithvi proved that India could master complex missile technology and laid the foundation for the nation’s rise as a major missile power.

14/14
tweets.content:
An Enduring Legacy

Though India is gradually replacing liquid-fueled Prithvi variants with faster-reacting, solid-fueled systems such as Prahaar and Pralay, the missile remains a landmark achievement. Produced in series by Bharat Dynamics Limited and deployed across multiple regiments, Prithvi proved that India could master complex missile technology and laid the foundation for the nation’s rise as a major missile power.

14/14
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: Beyond Strike Missions

Prithvi’s legacy extends far beyond offensive operations. Its airframe served as the basis for the Prithvi Air Defence (PAD) interceptor, a critical component of India’s Ballistic Missile Defence programme. Knowledge gained from Prithvi also contributed directly to the development of the Agni missile family.

13/14
tweets.content:
Beyond Strike Missions

Prithvi’s legacy extends far beyond offensive operations. Its airframe served as the basis for the Prithvi Air Defence (PAD) interceptor, a critical component of India’s Ballistic Missile Defence programme. Knowledge gained from Prithvi also contributed directly to the development of the Agni missile family.

13/14
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title:
THREAD 🧵 

The Birth of India’s Missile Revolution

In the early 1980s, India faced significant geopolitical vulnerabilities and heavy dependence on imported weapon systems. To achieve strategic autonomy, the Government of India launched the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP) in 1983 under the leadership of Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam. The programme aimed to establish a complete indigenous missile ecosystem.

1/14
tweets.content:
THREAD 🧵

The Birth of India’s Missile Revolution

In the early 1980s, India faced significant geopolitical vulnerabilities and heavy dependence on imported weapon systems. To achieve strategic autonomy, the Government of India launched the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP) in 1983 under the leadership of Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam. The programme aimed to establish a complete indigenous missile ecosystem.

1/14

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: Pakistan’s so-called strategic experts are as dumb as their DG-ISPR and their CDF.

Looks like @TanzeelaMirxa gets her information from social media, just like her Defence Minister @KhawajaMAsif, who has repeatedly presented social media posts as “evidence.”

There seems to be a competition underway in Pakistan over who can display the greatest level of incompetence between the military leadership, political slaves, so-called strategic experts, and journalists.

PS: India tested Agni-V with MIRV technology, but the so-called strategic expert thinks India tested Agni-VI. That’s what happens when social media becomes your primary source of information and the irony? She was handed a microphone to ask a factually incorrect question to @PeteHegseth at the Shangri-La Dialogue.

In India, people with this level of understanding wouldn’t be allowed to roam within 2 km of a major geopolitical event.

tweets.title: It is unfortunate that we may once again find ourselves buying the modern equivalent of our Jaguars ~ the Rafale. The underlying issue remains familiar: HF-24 Marut ( Tejas) struggling due to engine dependence, while HF-73's (Tejas Mk2 and AMCA) are still at the prototype and development stage. For some reason, we seem reluctant to learn from the lessons of our own aerospace history.
tweets.content:
It is unfortunate that we may once again find ourselves buying the modern equivalent of our Jaguars ~ the Rafale. The underlying issue remains familiar: HF-24 Marut ( Tejas) struggling due to engine dependence, while HF-73's (Tejas Mk2 and AMCA) are still at the prototype and development stage. For some reason, we seem reluctant to learn from the lessons of our own aerospace history.

From civilians to soldiers, from dreams to duty, these young men have earned the right to wear the uniform through hard work, discipline, and sacrifice.

May they always uphold the honour of their regiment, protect the nation with courage, and return safely to their loved ones.

Cryptorefills (@Cryptorefills)

The HTTP 402 status code was defined in 1992, reserved under the name "Payment Required," and left unimplemented for over thirty years. x402 is the first production-scale implementation of it. Cryptorefills is the first merchant to operate x402 across a consumer catalog.
